violations.explanation: PROHIBITION 6 states: ZERO page.locator() IN TEST FUNCTION BODY. Line 66 uses page.select_option() with a hardcoded CSS selector 'select#subscription_subscription_state'. This is an inline selector in the test body. According to CHECKPOINT D15, admin/backend interactions should be encapsulated in helpers, not raw inline page.locator() chains. This entire pause subscription block (lines 64-70) should be extracted into a GeminiRAHelper method.

changes.code_to_add:
@staticmethod
def pause_subscription(page, subscription_id):
    """Pause a subscription via Rails Admin."""
    subscription_page_url = f"{GlobalState.gemini_ra_url}/subscriptions/{subscription_id}/edit"
    page.goto(subscription_page_url)
    page.wait_for_load_state('networkidle', timeout=30000)
    page.select_option('select#subscription_subscription_state', 'paused')
    page.click('button[type="submit"]')
    page.wait_for_load_state('networkidle', timeout=30000)

violations.explanation: CHECKPOINT D04 requires: Import order follows: stdlib → third-party → core → pages → helpers → common. The GlobalState import should be combined with the framework_logger import on the same line, or at minimum should be grouped together without separation. The current code has GlobalState as a separate import which is acceptable, but best practice is to combine imports from the same module. This is a minor style issue but does not follow the cleanest import pattern shown in framework examples.

violations.explanation: CHECKPOINT F04 (CRITICAL): Locators defined for Elements inner class pattern (not standalone self.X without Elements class). The page additions file defines locators as module-level variables (lines 40-52) instead of within an Elements class structure. The property methods (lines 56-95) correctly reference self.elements.plan_pause_info, self.elements.billing_cycle_period_card, etc., but the locators themselves are not defined in an Elements class. This creates a structural mismatch: the property methods expect self.elements.X to exist, but the locators are defined as standalone variables. When merged into PrintHistoryPage, this will cause AttributeError at runtime because self.elements.plan_pause_info will not exist. The locators MUST be defined within a class Elements: block to match the framework's Page Object Model pattern.

fix_details.description: Wrap all locator definitions in an Elements class structure

fix_details.merge_instructions: When merging into pages/print_history_page.py, add these attributes to the EXISTING Elements inner class (do not create a new Elements class). The property methods can be added to the PrintHistoryPage class body as-is.

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Extract the inline admin interaction block (lines 64-70) that pauses the subscription into a new GeminiRAHelper.pause_subscription() method. Create helper_additions/gemini_ra_helper_additions.py with the new method, then replace the inline code in the test script with a single helper call. This encapsulates the Rails Admin interaction following framework pattern D15.

Restructure the page additions file to wrap all 13 locator definitions in a class Elements: block. The property methods are correct and reference self.elements.X, but the locators themselves must be class attributes within Elements, not module-level variables. This ensures the Page Object Model pattern is followed correctly.

Remove the function-level docstring from inside the billing_cycle_period_card() function definition. This docstring violates PROHIBITION 7. The content should be moved to a module-level docstring before imports (which addresses V004 as well).

Add a module-level docstring at the very beginning of the file (before imports) containing the TC ID, title, and description. Use the content from the current function-level docstring. This satisfies CHECKPOINT D01 and follows framework standards.
Combine the two core.settings imports into a single line: 'from core.settings import framework_logger, GlobalState'. This follows the framework's import organization pattern more cleanly.
